The bark, leaves, and fruit of Pyrus communis have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus communis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
